channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: Taliban Crack Down on Anti-Hijab Protest in Western Afghanistan
item.description: Afghan security forces dispersed a women's rights protest in Herat after Taliban morality police detained women for violating dress codes, marking another escalation in the regime's restrictions on women's freedoms.

item.title: Dollar Holds Steady as Markets Brace for US-Iran Tensions and Inflation Data
item.description: The US dollar remained stable against major currencies as investors monitored escalating tensions between Washington and Tehran while awaiting key inflation data that could influence Federal Reserve rate decisions.

item.title: Israel Launches Deadly Strikes on Lebanon as Trump Seeks Peace Deal
item.description: Israeli attacks killed at least 17 people in southern Lebanon after evacuation orders in Tyre, even as former President Trump claimed the US-Iran peace deal was in its "final throes" amid escalating regional tensions.

item.title: China Urges Restraint After US Strikes on Iran Raise Middle East Tensions
item.description: Beijing expressed deep concern over escalating Middle East conflict following US military strikes on Iran, calling for de-escalation as regional tensions surge after Washington's retaliation for the downing of an American helicopter.

item.title: Singapore PM Lawrence Wong to Visit Russia, First Trip Since Ukraine War Sanctions
item.description: Singapore's Prime Minister Lawrence Wong will travel to Russia for the first time since sanctions were imposed over the Ukraine war, signaling potential diplomatic shifts in Southeast Asian foreign policy.

item.title: Hong Kong Debt Market Surges as Tencent and Swire Raise $5.2 Billion in Multi-Currency Deals
item.description: Hong Kong's debt market showed renewed strength as major corporations raised billions in multi-currency bond issuances, reflecting investor confidence despite regional economic uncertainties.

item.title: Saudi Arabia Launches Riyadh Air Despite Regional War and Delays
item.description: Saudi Arabia's new national carrier Riyadh Air has officially launched operations despite challenges from regional conflicts and delays, marking a significant step in the kingdom's economic diversification plans.

item.title: Portable Nuclear Reactors Move Closer to Powering US Military Bases During Outages
item.description: Antares Nuclear has achieved a milestone in developing portable nuclear reactors designed to provide backup power to US military installations during grid outages, advancing energy resilience capabilities.

item.title: South Africa Mass Shooting: 12 Killed in Johannesburg Informal Settlement
item.description: Twelve people were killed and nine injured in a mass shooting at an informal settlement in Johannesburg, with police launching a manhunt for suspects in one of South Africa's deadliest recent attacks.

item.title: Ukraine Signs Drone Deal With Latvia as Zelenskiy Meets Nordic-Baltic Leaders
item.description: Ukraine signed a drone cooperation agreement with Latvia during meetings with Nordic-Baltic leaders, strengthening defense partnerships amid ongoing conflict with Russia.

item.title: Malaysia to Supply Maximum LNG and Naphtha to Japan Under New Agreement
item.description: Malaysia committed to providing maximum possible LNG and naphtha supplies to Japan under a new energy agreement, supporting Japan's efforts to diversify energy sources amid Middle East supply disruptions.

item.title: Senegal Football Federation Explains Viral Airport Security Footage Before World Cup
item.description: Senegal's football federation addressed viral security footage from their airport departure ahead of World Cup preparations, clarifying the incident to concerned fans.

item.title: Switzerland to Vote on Unprecedented Population Cap at 10 Million
item.description: Swiss voters will decide on a groundbreaking referendum to cap the country's population at 10 million, reflecting growing concerns about immigration and sustainability in the wealthy Alpine nation.

item.title: US Students Show First Gains in Reading and Math After Years of Decline
item.description: American students recorded their first improvements in reading and math scores following years of pandemic-related learning losses, according to new national assessment data showing tentative signs of academic recovery.

item.title: Iran Strikes US Bases in Jordan and Gulf States in Retaliation
item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps launched retaliatory strikes against US military bases in Jordan and Gulf states following American attacks near the Strait of Hormuz, escalating regional tensions.

item.title: Poland Demands Seat at Ukraine Peace Negotiations
item.description: Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk called for Poland's direct involvement in any Ukraine peace negotiations, emphasizing the country's security concerns and regional stake in the conflict's outcome.

item.title: Japan Wholesale Inflation Hits 3-Year High on Middle East Energy Shock
item.description: Japan's wholesale inflation reached a three-year high as energy costs surged following Middle East supply disruptions, adding pressure to the world's third-largest economy.

item.title: Bulgaria's New Government Ends Arms Support for Ukraine
item.description: Bulgaria's newly elected government announced it would cease providing military assistance to Ukraine, marking a shift in Eastern European support for Kyiv's defense efforts.

item.title: UK Charities Donated £28M to Illegal Israeli Settlements
item.description: Charities in England and Wales donated approximately £28 million to organizations supporting illegal Israeli settlements in the occupied West Bank, raising concerns about regulatory compliance and international law.

item.title: Taiwan test-fires US-supplied HIMARS toward China in first public display
item.description: Taiwan conducted its first public test-firing of US-supplied HIMARS rocket launchers toward China, demonstrating enhanced defensive capabilities amid heightened cross-strait tensions.

item.title: Protesters torch vehicles and buildings in Belfast after refugee knife attack
item.description: Northern Ireland's capital saw violent protests after a refugee was allegedly involved in a knife attack, with demonstrators setting fires to vehicles and buildings in sectarian unrest.

item.title: China prepares $295 billion AI infrastructure plan to challenge US dominance
item.description: China is developing a massive $295 billion investment plan to build nationwide AI infrastructure, aiming to compete with US technological supremacy in artificial intelligence development.

item.title: Bill Gates to face US Congress questioning over Epstein links
item.description: Bill Gates will testify before the US Congress regarding his connections to Jeffrey Epstein, as lawmakers seek answers about the late financier's relationships with prominent figures.

item.title: IRGC Claims Strikes on US Bases in Jordan, Kuwait and Bahrain
item.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps claimed responsibility for strikes targeting US military installations across Jordan, Kuwait, and Bahrain in retaliation for American attacks on Iranian territory.

item.title: Kuwait Intercepts Hostile Aerial Targets Amid Regional Escalation
item.description: Kuwait's air defense systems intercepted hostile aerial targets as regional tensions escalated following US-Iran military exchanges, with Gulf states on high alert.
